rationale: The citing paper uses Pocheptsova et al. (2009) for a specific theoretical function, not just as a background list citation. In the general discussion, it cites that study as evidence that resource depletion attenuates the compromise effect, and uses that evidence to argue that the compromise effect depends on deliberative processing. This point helps the authors distinguish their own mechanism (analytical vs affective processing under money vs time) from a simple heuristic-processing account.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's depletion finding as convergent evidence about the processing basis of the compromise effect. It uses that result to support its interpretive argument about why its own money-vs-time effects should not be reduced to heuristic use.
